title: 10 Shows Like Elite: Your Next Binge-Worthy Teen Drama & Mystery
metaDescription: Craving more high-stakes teen drama, murder mysteries, and class conflict? Discover 10 shows similar to Netflix's Elite, from Spanish hits to global favorites, for your next binge.
content:
Since its explosive debut in 2018, Netflix’s Elite has captivated audiences worldwide with its intoxicating blend of high-stakes teen drama, tantalizing mysteries, and sharp social commentary. Set within the opulent halls of Las Encinas, Spain’s most exclusive private school, the series masterfully weaves together narratives of privilege, class conflict, illicit affairs, and, inevitably, murder. Each season delivers a fresh mystery, often framed by a flash-forward investigation, keeping viewers on the edge of their seats as they try to unravel who did what, and why.

As we navigate through 2026, with Elite having cemented its legacy as a global phenomenon, many fans find themselves yearning for that same rush of adrenaline, that intricate web of secrets, and those compelling, often morally ambiguous characters. If you’ve devoured every season of Elite and are searching for your next obsession, you’re in luck. We’ve curated a list of 10 incredible shows that capture the essence of what makes Elite so addictive, offering everything from more Spanish-language sensations to international thrillers and gritty teen dramas. Prepare your watchlist, because your next binge is about to begin!

The Allure of Las Encinas: Why We Love Elite

What makes Elite so uniquely compelling? It’s more than just a “whodunit” wrapped in a glamorous package. The show expertly dissects the inherent tensions between the “old money” elite and “new blood” scholarship students, exposing the hypocrisy and dark underbelly of extreme wealth. The characters, despite their flaws and often outrageous actions, are deeply human, grappling with identity, sexuality, love, and loyalty. The non-linear storytelling, with its signature flash-forwards to police interrogations, creates an irresistible puzzle, making every episode a piece of a larger, deadly mosaic. Plus, let’s be honest, the fashion, the parties, and the undeniable chemistry between the cast members are simply irresistible. If you’re looking for even more details on Elite, including character breakdowns and episode guides, remember you can find extensive resources right here on SpanishTVShows.com.

Spanish-Speaking Sensations: Keeping the Drama Local

If you love the original language and cultural nuances of Elite, these Spanish-language series are perfect for keeping the vibe alive.

1. Rebelde (Netflix)

Plot: A reboot of the iconic Mexican telenovela, Netflix’s Rebelde transports viewers back to the prestigious Elite Way School, a private boarding school known for its rigorous arts program. A new generation of students arrives, all with their own ambitions, secrets, and a desire to win the coveted Battle of the Bands. However, an underground secret society known as “The Lodge” threatens to destroy their musical dreams and uncover their deepest secrets.

Connection to Elite: Rebelde shares Elite’s core DNA: a high-end private school setting, a cast of attractive, wealthy teens, and an undercurrent of mystery and danger. While perhaps less overtly dark than Elite, it still features intense teen drama, complex romantic entanglements, class struggles (especially between scholarship students and the privileged), and a secret society pulling strings from the shadows. The characters’ personal lives are just as complicated, filled with betrayals, friendships, and the pursuit of truth, all against a backdrop of musical ambition.

2. Control Z (Netflix)

Plot: Set in a Mexican high school, Control Z kicks off when a mysterious hacker exposes the deepest secrets of several students to the entire school. Suddenly, popular jock Luis is outed, and “it girl” Sofi’s manipulative past is revealed, among others. Sofi, a keen observer with a knack for deduction, takes it upon herself to unmask the hacker, leading her down a perilous path that uncovers more secrets, lies, and eventually, violence.

Connection to Elite: This show is a near-perfect match for Elite fans. It features a high school setting, a central mystery that drives the plot, and a cast of diverse characters whose lives are interconnected through secrets and lies. Like Elite, Control Z explores themes of social status, bullying, revenge, and the devastating consequences of secrets being exposed. The tension escalates with each revelation, culminating in shocking twists and often violent outcomes, mirroring Elite’s own high-stakes narrative and the constant threat of danger lurking beneath the surface of seemingly perfect lives. For more on this gripping series, check out its dedicated page on SpanishTVShows.com.

3. Valeria (Netflix)

Plot: Based on the popular novels by Elísabet Benavent, Valeria follows a writer in Madrid who is struggling with her marriage, her career, and her self-confidence. Her three best friends — Carmen, Lola, and Nerea — are her constant support system, each navigating their own romantic relationships, professional challenges, and personal crises. The series is a candid and often humorous look at female friendship, love, sex, and the messy reality of being a woman in your late twenties/early thirties.

Connection to Elite: While Valeria is decidedly “New Adult” rather than “Teen Drama,” fans of Elite will appreciate its focus on intricate relationship dynamics, passionate love triangles, and the raw exploration of sexuality and personal struggles. Both shows excel at crafting compelling character arcs and showcasing the complexities of friendships tested by jealousy, ambition, and secrets. If you enjoy Elite’s mature themes and character-driven drama, but are looking for a slightly older perspective on life’s challenges, Valeria offers a fantastic, equally binge-worthy experience, albeit with less murder and more existential angst.

4. Élite: Historias Breves (Netflix)

Plot: These short-form mini-series serve as interstitial episodes between seasons of the main Elite show, offering glimpses into the lives of various characters during summer breaks or holidays. Each “Historias Breves” focuses on a few specific characters, exploring their personal dilemmas, unresolved tensions, or new relationships in a more intimate, concentrated format. They often fill in gaps, provide backstory, or set the stage for upcoming seasons.

Connection to Elite: This is the most direct connection possible! If you love Elite, these short stories are essential viewing. They deepen your understanding of the characters you already adore (or love to hate), offering more character development, relationship insights, and often, lighter or more focused drama than the main series. They provide that extra fix of Las Encinas gossip, romance, and class conflict that fans crave, making the wait between full seasons a little more bearable. It’s like getting bonus episodes of your favorite show.

Global High School Mayhem: International Picks

Elite proved that teen drama with a dark edge transcends borders. These international selections bring similar thrills from various corners of the globe.

5. Gossip Girl (HBO Max)

Plot: The 2021 reboot of the iconic series brings us back to the exclusive world of New York City’s Upper East Side. A new generation of privileged private school teens — including influencers, socialites, and fashionistas — finds their lives under the scrutiny of a revived “Gossip Girl” Instagram account. This mysterious entity once again exposes their secrets, scandals, and lies, forcing them to confront the consequences of their actions in the digital age.

Connection to Elite: Gossip Girl is arguably one of Elite’s spiritual predecessors, and the reboot continues that tradition with a modern twist. Both shows are steeped in the world of extreme wealth, private school hierarchies, and the constant threat of secrets being revealed. The “Gossip Girl” persona acts as a similar catalyst for drama and mystery as the murder investigations in Elite, forcing characters to confront their hidden lives. Expect lavish parties, intricate romantic entanglements, backstabbing friendships, and a constant struggle for social dominance, all set against a backdrop of undeniable style and privilege.

6. Blood & Water (Netflix)

Plot: Set in Cape Town, South Africa, Blood & Water follows Puleng Khumalo, a smart and determined teenager who transfers to the elite Parkhurst College, a prestigious private school for the wealthy. Her mission? To investigate the cold case of her older sister, Phume, who was abducted at birth and whom Puleng suspects is one of Parkhurst’s star students, Fikile Bhele. As Puleng delves deeper, she uncovers a complex web of family secrets, illicit activities, and dangerous connections within the school’s privileged circles.

Connection to Elite: This South African gem is a must-watch for Elite fans. It nails the private school setting, the “outsider infiltrating the elite” trope, and a central, gripping mystery that unravels over multiple seasons. Like Elite, it features a diverse cast, explores themes of class disparity, identity, and the lengths people will go to protect their secrets. The show is packed with twists, turns, and high emotional stakes, making it incredibly binge-worthy and reminiscent of Elite’s own blend of teen drama and thrilling investigative narrative.

7. Baby (Netflix)

Plot: Inspired by a true scandal in Rome, Italy, Baby follows two wealthy, privileged high school girls, Chiara and Ludovica, who become entangled in the city’s underground world of prostitution. Driven by boredom, rebellion, and a desire for independence from their suffocating upper-class lives, they navigate a dangerous double life, balancing school, friendships, and their secret nocturnal activities. The series explores their complex relationship, their struggles with identity, and the moral ambiguities of their choices.

Connection to Elite: Baby shares Elite’s fascination with the dark side of privilege and the rebellious impulses of youth. Both shows feature wealthy, attractive teenagers pushing boundaries, engaging in illicit activities, and dealing with the consequences of their secrets. The intense friendships, complicated romantic relationships, and the moral decay beneath a polished surface are strong parallels. While Baby focuses more on the psychological and social ramifications of its central secret rather than a murder mystery, it offers the same kind of gripping, mature teen drama that makes Elite so compelling.

8. The Wilds (Amazon Prime Video)

Plot: A group of teenage girls from vastly different backgrounds are left stranded on a remote island after a plane crash, seemingly by accident. As they struggle to survive, they uncover a shocking truth: their “accident” was actually a meticulously planned social experiment. Flashbacks reveal the personal struggles and traumas each girl faced before the crash, providing context for their behavior and alliances on the island as they fight for survival and uncover the sinister nature of their predicament.

Connection to Elite: While set in a survival scenario rather than a high school, The Wilds masterfully employs Elite’s signature elements: a core mystery (what happened, and why?), a diverse group of complex teenagers forced into close quarters, and a reliance on flashbacks to reveal character depth and plot twists. The show explores themes of class, trauma, identity, and the raw dynamics of human relationships under extreme pressure. The constant tension, the unraveling of secrets, and the high-stakes survival plot will appeal to anyone who loves Elite’s blend of character-driven drama and thrilling mystery.

Beyond the Classroom: Adult Mysteries & Thrillers with a Teen Edge

Sometimes, the “teen” part of the drama isn’t strictly necessary, as long as the mystery, twists, and high stakes remain. These shows offer a slightly more adult perspective while retaining Elite’s addictive qualities.

9. How to Get Away with Murder (ABC/Netflix)

Plot: Annalise Keating, a brilliant, charismatic, and morally ambiguous criminal defense professor, selects a group of ambitious law students — known as the “Keating 5” — to intern at her firm. Their lives quickly spiral into chaos when they become entangled in a murder plot, forcing them to use their legal knowledge to cover up their crimes. The series is famous for its intricate, non-linear storytelling, utilizing flash-forwards to reveal shocking events and then meticulously filling in the gaps.

Connection to Elite: This show is perhaps the closest parallel to Elite’s narrative structure, especially its use of flash-forwards to a central murder mystery. Both shows excel at building suspense around “who did it” and “how did they get away with it.” The “Keating 5” often feel like older versions of the Las Encinas students, bound by a shared secret and forced to make increasingly desperate choices. It’s packed with betrayals, shocking twists, and morally gray characters, making it an ideal binge for anyone who loves Elite’s darker, more complex crime elements.

10. Euphoria (HBO)

Plot: Euphoria delves into the often-disturbing lives of a group of high school students in East Highland, California, as they navigate love, loss, trauma, addiction, and friendship. Led by Rue Bennett, a recovering drug addict, the series offers a raw, unflinching, and visually stunning portrayal of contemporary adolescence. It explores the pressures of social media, identity, sexuality, and the search for meaning in a chaotic world, often through stylized and intense sequences.

Connection to Elite: While Euphoria doesn’t feature a central murder mystery like Elite, it shares the Spanish series’ commitment to exploring the darker, more complex aspects of teenage life with unflinching honesty. Both shows feature gorgeous cinematography, a compelling soundtrack, and a cast of deeply flawed yet magnetic characters. If you appreciate Elite’s mature themes, its exploration of identity and sexuality, and its willingness to delve into the consequences of reckless behavior, Euphoria provides an equally intense, character-driven experience, albeit with a focus more on psychological drama and social commentary.

Your Next Obsession Awaits

Whether you’re drawn to the intricate mysteries, the scandalous affairs, the class warfare, or simply the irresistible charm of its young, talented cast, Elite has set a high bar for teen drama. Fortunately, as we’ve seen, the streaming landscape in 2026 is rich with shows that offer similar thrills and spills. From more Spanish-language hits like Control Z and Rebelde to international sensations like Blood & Water and the compelling adult mysteries of How to Get Away with Murder, there’s a whole world of high-stakes drama waiting for you.

So, grab your snacks, settle in, and prepare to lose yourself in another captivating world of secrets, scandal, and unforgettable characters. And for even more recommendations and in-depth analyses of your favorite Spanish-language series, don’t forget to explore the extensive content available right here on SpanishTVShows.com. Happy binge-watching!
author: SpanishTVShows Editorial
